sv.ref: Baldwin — Practical Guide to Sous Vide (poultry pasteurization)
sv.note: Stacked turkey thigh (dark meat) on a vertical spit. 74°C instantly exceeds every cell of Baldwin's poultry table (65°C/50mm = 2.25h); the long 12h hold is for collagen/tendon breakdown so slices carve tender, not for extra safety.

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — turkey smoke restraint (smoke is a seasoning)
smoke.note: Post-SV hot smoke for bark; Meathead: use fruit wood sparingly on turkey. tgt 75°C keeps dark meat above the 74°C floor after the 74°C bath.

smoke_only.ref: AmazingRibs — dark poultry to 170-175°F for collagen
smoke_only.note: Low-and-slow raw spit to internal 75°C+; dark meat benefits from the extra time in the 160→175°F band to melt connective tissue.

safe.note: Displayed poultry floor 74°C (165°F). Bath already at 74°C so meat is pasteurized instantly per Baldwin's table (a 1e7:1 Salmonella reduction is reached far below this).

rationale: Kept all values. 74°C/12h is a pasteurize-and-tenderize dark-meat cook (Baldwin) finished with light fruit-wood smoke (Meathead); safe stays at the 74°C poultry floor.

sv.note: Boneless thigh ~30mm: Baldwin 65°C/30mm = 1h15 to pasteurize, so 2h gives full Salmonella safety plus a tender dark-meat texture (AmazingRibs SV-que thighs = 148°F/65°C).

smoke.note: After the bath, ~30 min hot smoke adds flavor with little temp rise. Oak/apple are moderate; Meathead warns hickory/pecan can overpower chicken.

smoke_only.ref: AmazingRibs — smoke chicken at 325°F for skin
smoke_only.note: Raw thighs at ~130°C (≈265°F) to internal 75°C; dark meat is even better carried toward 175°F/79°C for collagen if you want fall-apart texture.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C. The 65°C bath pasteurizes by TIME (Baldwin table), but the grill/smoke-only path has no long hold, so displayed floor stays 74°C.

rationale: Kept values; 65°C/2h is well past Baldwin's 65°C/30mm=1h15 pasteurization point and matches Meathead's 148°F thigh. Safe held at 74°C floor.

sv.note: Labeled goose breast (חזה אווז). Leaner/gamier than duck, so cooked a touch past duck medium-rare: 63°C = medium. Baldwin 63°C/30mm = 1.5h, so 3h fully pasteurizes.

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — fruit wood for waterfowl breast
smoke.note: Clean cherry smoke; short so core stays at 65°C. tgt 65°C keeps this intact whole-muscle breast at a rosy medium.

smoke_only.ref: AmazingRibs — keep waterfowl cooker hot to render fat/crisp skin
smoke_only.note: Score skin; ≈110°C then hot blast to crisp. Pull breast ~65°C to avoid chewy overcooking.

safe.ref: Baldwin poultry floor — but intact whole-muscle exception noted
safe.note: Displayed floor stays 74°C for the app's grill-only path. tgt 65°C is intentionally below it: goose/duck breast is intact whole muscle (interior sterile, surface seared), so served medium is standard and safe like red meat. Do NOT raise tgt to 74.

rationale: Kept values. Whole-muscle waterfowl breast exception: low tgt 65°C (medium) is correct and safe; displayed safe stays at the 74°C poultry floor per policy.

sv.note: Thick (≈50mm) turkey breast: Baldwin 65°C/50mm = 2.25h to pasteurize, so 3h is a safe margin while keeping white meat juicy at 65°C.

smoke.note: Meathead: white meat ≈160°F/71°C, rest to 165°F/74°C. tgt 75°C keeps it above floor; cherry/apple, used sparingly.

smoke_only.note: sot 110°C is a low-slow choice to internal 75°C; Meathead actually prefers ~325°F/163°C for better skin and a shorter time in the danger zone — consider raising for skin.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C. 65°C bath pasteurizes by time (Baldwin) but is documented in-note, not lowered, because the smoke-only path lacks the long hold.

rationale: Kept values; 65°C/3h exceeds Baldwin's 65°C/50mm=2.25h pasteurization and matches Meathead's 160→165°F carryover. Safe held at 74°C floor.

sv.ref: Baldwin — poultry pasteurized at 60-65°C by time
sv.note: Whole spatchcocked bird; thickest breast ≈40mm: Baldwin 65°C/50mm = 2.25h, so 2-3h pasteurizes throughout.

smoke.note: Meathead cooks spatchcock at ~325°F/163°C to breast 160°F and thigh 175°F; smt 130°C hot-finish + tgt 74°C aligns. 2-3 chunks apple/oak.

smoke_only.ref: AmazingRibs — 325°F for spatchcock, ~1 h to 160°F breast
smoke_only.note: 150°C (≈300-325°F) raw to internal 74°C; spatchcocking evens cooking so breast finishes near thigh.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C; whole bird includes surfaces and joints so the conservative instant-safe number is kept.

rationale: Kept values; smoke temps and 160°F breast / 175°F thigh targets match Meathead's spatchcock recipe, SV pasteurizes per Baldwin, safe at 74°C floor.

sv.note: Wings are thin (≤20mm): Baldwin 65°C/20mm = 40 min, so 1-2h fully pasteurizes and renders the dark meat.

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — wings need high heat for crispy skin
smoke.note: Pitmasters run wings at ~350°F/177°C to crisp skin in 45-60 min; smt 150°C hot-finish with apple/cherry gets there.

smoke_only.note: Higher is better for wing skin; ~150-177°C to internal 74°C. Cornstarch/baking-powder dry brine aids crisping.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C; kept conservative for the smoke-only path.

rationale: Kept values; thin wings pasteurize fast (Baldwin) and finish hot (~350°F) for crispy skin per Meathead. Safe at 74°C floor.

sv.ref: Baldwin — poultry table; dark meat wants higher
sv.note: Drumstick ≈35mm dark meat: 70°C pasteurizes in well under 1h (above Baldwin's 65°C row) and starts collagen conversion so 2h yields tender, moist meat.

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — dark meat to 175°F for render/crisp
smoke.note: Meathead: legs best ~175°F/79°C to render fat and crisp skin; tgt 80°C matches. Smoke at ~325°F/130-163°C.

smoke_only.note: 140°C (≈285°F) raw to internal 80°C; the extended 160→175°F band liquefies connective tissue.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C; tgt 80°C already exceeds it for dark-meat texture.

rationale: Kept values; 70°C/2h over-pasteurizes and tgt 80°C matches Meathead's 175°F dark-meat target. Safe at 74°C floor.

sv.note: Raised svt 63→65°C: Meathead directly tested and settled on 150°F (65.6°C) as the best moisture/texture balance. Raised svh floor to 1.5h: a 0.4kg breast is ~30-40mm and Baldwin needs 65°C/30mm = 1h15 (63°C/30mm = 1.5h), so the old 1h lower bound was borderline for Salmonella.

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — SV-que breast, quick sear/smoke finish
smoke.note: After the bath, ~2 min/side direct/smoke; core barely rises. tgt 68°C (≈155°F) keeps white meat just-done and juicy. Cherry/apple.

smoke_only.note: Raw breast at ~120°C to internal 74°C on the no-SV path; keep it short so white meat doesn't dry.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C. tgt 68°C is below it because the 65°C bath pasteurizes by time (Baldwin), but the smoke-only path has no long hold, so the displayed floor stays 74°C.

rationale: CHANGED sv: svt 63→65°C to match Meathead's tested 150°F optimum, and svh 1-2→1.5-2 so the lower bound clears Baldwin's pasteurization time for a 30-40mm breast. Safe held at 74°C floor.

sv.ref: Baldwin — poultry pasteurized; long hold for tendons
sv.note: Turkey legs are dense dark meat full of tendons: 74°C pasteurizes instantly, and 8-12h is needed to convert collagen so the meat pulls cleanly off the bone.

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — Disney-style legs, dark meat to ~180°F
smoke.note: Post-SV smoke for the classic pink-ringed leg; tgt 85°C (≈185°F) fully renders tendons. Oak/apple.

smoke_only.ref: AmazingRibs — legs need 3-4h for collagen to melt
smoke_only.note: ≈120°C raw to internal 85°C; legs take 3-4h low-and-slow to become tender.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C; the 74°C bath meets it directly and tgt 85°C exceeds it.

rationale: Kept values; 74°C/8-12h pasteurizes and breaks down tendons, tgt 85°C matches Meathead's dark-meat leg finish. Safe at 74°C floor.

sv.ref: Baldwin — poultry pasteurized by time at 63°C
sv.note: Whole bird (usually parted for SV); thick breast ≈50mm: Baldwin 63°C/50mm = 2.5h, so 4-5h pasteurizes even the thickest section.

smoke.note: Meathead: cook at 325°F, pull ~160°F, carryover to 165°F/74°C; thigh ~170°F. Use apple/cherry sparingly ('nobody ruined a turkey by under-smoking it').

smoke_only.ref: AmazingRibs — 325°F is the sweet spot for whole turkey
smoke_only.note: sot 150°C (≈300-325°F) to internal 74°C; hot enough to crisp skin and shorten the danger-zone window on a big bird.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C; conservative instant-safe kept for the whole-bird smoke-only path (surfaces, cavity, joints).

rationale: Kept values; smoke temp and 160°F breast/170°F thigh targets match Meathead's whole-turkey guide, SV pasteurizes per Baldwin. Safe at 74°C floor.

sv.note: Fatty whole goose; 64°C is just above Baldwin's 63°C poultry row (63°C/50mm = 2.5h), so 6h pasteurizes and begins fat softening.

smoke.ref: ThermoWorks/Meathead — waterfowl: keep cooker hot, fruit wood
smoke.note: Prick skin to drain the heavy fat; breast to ~165°F/74°C. Dark meat is better toward 175°F/79°C to fully render — pull breast earlier than legs. Apple/cherry.

smoke_only.ref: Dive Bomb / ThermoWorks — smoke goose ~300°F, render fat 3x
smoke_only.note: Keep it hotter (nearer 300°F) not 225°F to crisp skin; drain rendered fat several times. Internal 74°C breast, 79°C legs.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C; conservative instant-safe kept for the whole-bird.

rationale: Kept values; SV pasteurizes per Baldwin and smoke plan (hot cooker, fruit wood, drain fat, 165°F breast) matches ThermoWorks/goose sources. Safe at 74°C floor.

sv.note: Baldwin explicitly gives duck/goose leg confit at 176°F/80°C for 8-12h; 8-10h is squarely in range for tender, shreddable confit.

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — confit finish: hot indirect + sear to crisp skin
smoke.note: After the confit bath, ~325°F indirect then a hard sear crisps the skin; tgt 85°C is already reached in the bath. Oak.

smoke_only.ref: AmazingRibs — waterfowl legs low-and-slow to render
smoke_only.note: ≈120°C to internal 85°C; long enough to render fat and melt connective tissue on the leg.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C; the 80°C confit bath and tgt 85°C far exceed it.

rationale: Kept values; matches Baldwin's 80°C/8-12h confit spec exactly, finished hot to crisp skin. Safe at 74°C floor.

sv.note: 64°C just above Baldwin's 63°C row (63°C/50mm = 2.5h); 4-5h pasteurizes the parted bird and softens the fat layer.

smoke.note: Keep cooker hot (300-325°F/149-163°C) so the fat renders and skin crisps; breast to 165°F/74°C. smt 130°C matches. Apple/cherry.

smoke_only.note: sot 140°C (≈285°F) to internal 74°C; score and prick skin to drain fat. Too low (225°F) leaves skin flabby.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C (165°F) — ThermoWorks notes 157°F is a common lower target but the USDA poultry minimum is 165°F; kept conservative for the whole bird.

rationale: Kept values; smoke temp (300-325°F), ~2.5h, 165°F breast target match ThermoWorks/Meathead duck guidance, SV pasteurizes per Baldwin. Safe at 74°C floor.

sv.note: Baldwin's confit spec is 176°F/80°C for 8-12h; 8h yields tender leg. (Meathead's alternative low route is 145°F/62.8°C for 24h — either works; this uses the traditional hot-confit temp.)

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — sous-vide-que duck confit finish (325°F + sear)
smoke.note: Finish ~325°F indirect + skin-down sear ~4 min to crisp; core already at 85°C from the bath. Oak.

smoke_only.ref: AmazingRibs — waterfowl leg low-and-slow to render
smoke_only.note: ≈120°C to internal 85°C; renders fat and melts collagen without the confit bath.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C; the 80°C bath and tgt 85°C far exceed it.

rationale: Kept values; matches Baldwin's 80°C/8-12h confit spec, finished hot to crisp skin (Meathead). Safe at 74°C floor.

sv.ref: ChefSteps — duck breast 135°F/57°C, ~2h; Serious Eats/Anova agree
sv.note: 57°C (135°F) is the standard medium-rare duck breast; ChefSteps '135°F' and Anova medium-rare recipe both use ~2h. Intact whole muscle, so served medium-rare like red meat.

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — pull duck breast 140-143°F, no higher (chewy above)
smoke.note: Very short/cool smoke so the rosy 57°C core is preserved; overcooking past ~150°F makes duck breast chewy. Oak.

smoke_only.ref: AmazingRibs — score skin, render, keep breast rosy
smoke_only.note: Hot-and-fast ≈120°C, ~45 min, pull core ~57°C; sear skin to crisp. Interior is whole muscle so medium-rare is safe.

safe.ref: Baldwin poultry floor — intact whole-muscle exception noted
safe.note: Displayed floor stays 74°C for the app's grill-only path, but tgt 57°C is intentionally far below it: duck breast is intact whole muscle (interior sterile, surface seared), so medium-rare is standard and safe like a steak. Do NOT raise tgt to the poultry floor.

rationale: Kept values. Whole-muscle duck-breast exception: 57°C/135°F medium-rare (ChefSteps/Serious Eats) is correct and safe; displayed safe stays at the 74°C poultry floor per policy.

sv.ref: Baldwin — poultry table; dark meat higher temp
sv.note: Leg quarter dark meat: 70°C is above Baldwin's 65°C row so it pasteurizes in well under 1h; 2h begins collagen conversion for tenderness.

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — legs/quarters to ~175°F for render & crisp skin
smoke.note: Meathead: dark meat best ~175°F/79°C; tgt 80°C matches. Smoke ~325°F/130-163°C; apple/cherry (avoid strong hickory).

smoke_only.note: 140°C (≈285°F) raw to internal 80°C; the 160→175°F band renders connective tissue.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C; tgt 80°C already exceeds it for dark-meat texture.

rationale: Kept values; 70°C/2h over-pasteurizes and tgt 80°C matches Meathead's 175°F dark-meat target. Safe at 74°C floor.

sv.ref: Baldwin — poultry pasteurized; hold for tendons
sv.note: Turkey wings are sinewy dark meat: 74°C pasteurizes instantly and 4h softens tendons so they eat tender.

smoke.ref: AmazingRibs — dark poultry to ~175-180°F for render
smoke.note: tgt 80°C (≈176°F) renders connective tissue; post-SV smoke for flavor. Apple/pecan (turkey handles a bit more smoke than chicken).

smoke_only.ref: AmazingRibs — dark turkey needs 3-4h for collagen
smoke_only.note: ≈130°C raw to internal 80°C; several hours needed for the tendon-heavy wing to become tender.

safe.note: Displayed floor 74°C; the 74°C bath meets it and tgt 80°C exceeds it.

rationale: Kept values; 74°C/4h pasteurizes and softens tendons, tgt 80°C matches dark-meat render. Safe at 74°C floor.
